rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2022 line 1. RESPONDENT JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA, VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to May 19, 2022 on Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Renewed) is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and Defendant failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Defendant's claims of authorized occupancy let alone tenancy at a housing project unit regulated by HUD are not supported by any evidence that could give rise to a triable issue of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
